architecture_decisions.decision: Implement Origin validation middleware in audit service WebSocket upgrader
architecture_decisions.rationale: Prevents Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) attacks by verifying the Origin header against a whitelist of approved origins
Provides single source of truth for WebSocket security settings, enables environment-specific configuration

architecture_decisions.decision: Add configurable origin validator with development defaults
architecture_decisions.rationality: Ensures development productivity while enforcing production security

architecture_decisions.decision: Implement structured logging for connection attempts
architecture_decisions.rationale: Provides audit trail for security monitoring and forensic analysis

backend_tasks.purpose: Add AuditAllowedOrigins configuration field with environment variable binding
Audit-specific configuration wrapper with origin validation logic
Add origin validation to WebSocket upgrader
WebSocket connection manager with origin tracking
Reusable middleware for HTTP endpoint origin validation
Admin API WebSocket middleware with origin validation
Wire up configuration and initialize EventStreamer with allowed origins
Terraform variables for audit service configuration
Terraform configuration for audit service deployment

frontend_tasks.purpose: Add explicit origin handling and connection error display
Add WebSocket connection helper with origin header
Display connection status and origin validation feedback
Zustand store for WebSocket connection state management

database_migrations.sql:
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS audit_stream_connections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    origin TEXT NOT NULL,
    client_ip INET NOT NULL,
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    connected_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
    disconnected_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    events_received INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',
    INDEX idx_origin (origin),
    INDEX idx_user_id (user_id),
    INDEX idx_connected_at (connected_at)
);

e2e_tests.name: WebSocket Connection with Valid Origin
steps: Configure audit service with allowed origin
Connect WebSocket with valid Origin header

e2e_tests.name: WebSocket Connection Rejected with Invalid Origin
steps: Configure audit service with specific origin
Attempt WebSocket connection with different Origin header

e2e_tests.name: Production Environment Validation
steps: Verify service rejects configuration or uses safe defaults

security_notes: CSWSH (Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking) vulnerability mitigated by strict Origin validation
Development mode allows localhost origins for convenience
Production mode requires explicit origin configuration; empty list is rejected
All connection attempts (accepted and rejected) are logged to audit_stream_connections table
Origin validation occurs BEFORE WebSocket handshake upgrade to minimize attack surface
Consider adding rate limiting per origin to prevent DoS
Monitor for suspicious connection patterns (repeated rejected origins)
Consider implementing Origin-Allowlist refresh without restart in future version
docker_changes: Add OPENIDX_AUDIT_ALLOWED_ORIGINS environment variable to audit-service container
Add health check endpoint for WebSocket availability
Document required production configuration in docker-compose README
market_driven_features: Conditional access checks before allowing audit stream connections (FR-M008)
User identity verification required for privileged audit access (FR-M011)
Device trust validation before establishing WebSocket connection (FR-M010)
Audit stream connection events logged for compliance reporting (FR-M014)
